notes: VERIFIED. Team Rocket (base5), WotC era (English release 2000-04-24, pre-Legendary-Collection). All cited sources re-fetched and confirm the figures. Bulbapedia Booster_pack page confirms 11 cards/pack from Base Set through Neo Destiny, era slot structure (>=1 rare, generally 3 uncommons, rest commons), 36 packs/box, and that reverse holos were FIRST introduced in Legendary Collection AFTER Team Rocket (so NO reverse-holo slot here). Flipside Gaming confirms early-era four rarities with holo ~1:3 packs and Dark Raichu secret-rare community pull-rate estimate of ~1:54-108 packs. Bulbapedia Team Rocket page confirms 83-card set with Dark Raichu as the FIRST Western secret card (83/82). GameRant confirms Dark Raichu is the secret rare / top chase card but gives NO numeric odds (so it corroborates existence, not the rate — rate sourcing rests on Flipside Gaming's 1:54-108 community range). Single rare slot modeled as: rare(non-holo)=0.667, holo-or-better=0.333 (matches the ~1:3 holo rate printed on packs); secret-rare drawn from the holo share at ~1:90 (0.011, mid-range of the cited 1:54-108 band ~0.0093-0.0185), leaving rare-holo=0.322. Slot sums exact: 0.667+0.322+0.011=1.000. Distribution keys are a strict subset of available buckets. Hit bucket = rare-holo. confidence=low: no per-set scientific pull study exists for this vintage set; rates are era-average plus community estimate applied to this set's buckets. Vintage sealed boxes carry a large collector premium, so EV/booster is expected to sit far below box price — rates are NOT inflated to compensate. No ETB existed in this era (displayPacks=36, etbPacks=null). Removed the PSA registry URL from the draft's source list since it was not load-bearing for any hit-rate figure and was not re-fetched; the four retained URLs were all fetched and support the structure/rate claims.
